turns.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="512" height="512" fill="#f0f0f0"/><rect x="80" y="120" width="350" height="270" fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="3" rx="10"/><rect x="80" y="120" width="350" height="50" fill="#333" rx="10"/><circle cx="100" cy="145" r="6" fill="#ff6b6b"/><circle cx="120" cy="145" r="6" fill="#ffd93d"/><circle cx="140" cy="145" r="6" fill="#6bcf7f"/><line x1="100" y1="200" x2="180" y2="200" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="100" y1="220" x2="220" y2="220" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="100" y1="240" x2="200" y2="240" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="100" y1="260" x2="180" y2="260" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="100" y1="280" x2="210" y2="280" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="100" y1="300" x2="190" y2="300" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="100" y1="320" x2="170" y2="320" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="100" y1="340" x2="220" y2="340" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="280,200 320,180 360,200 340,240 300,240" fill="none" stroke="#ff6b6b" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="280,280 320,260 360,280 340,320 300,320" fill="none" stroke="#6bcf7f" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="380" y1="200" x2="420" y2="240" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="420" y1="200" x2="380" y2="240" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/></svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
